title: VJ Edgecombe Has Historic Rookie Playoff Performance As Sixers Take Game 2 Over Celtics
summary: VJ Edgecombe delivered one of the most remarkable rookie playoff performances in NBA history Tuesday, scoring 30 points and grabbing 10 rebounds while battling a back injury as the Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Boston Celtics 111-97 to level their first-round series at 1-1. At 20 years and 265 days old, Edgecombe became the youngest player in NBA history to record 30 points and 10 rebounds in a playoff game, surpassing Magic Johnson, who was 20 years and 276 days old during his celebrated 42-point, 15-rebound showing in Game 6 of the 1980 NBA Finals. Edgecombe is also the first rookie to post those numbers in a playoff game since Tim Duncan's 33-point, 10-rebound effort in 1998. Tyrese Maxey added 29 points and nine assists for Philadelphia, which shot 47.8 percent from the field and connected on 19-of-39 three-point attempts. Edgecombe knocked down six of those triples. "I think we knew where the shots were going to come from," Edgecombe said. "We kept trusting each other. Everyone can make a play on the court. They wanted me to shoot the ball." The performance was made more striking by the circumstances. Edgecombe took a hard fall early and left for the locker room in visible discomfort twice, once in the first quarter and again in the third, before returning both times to continue scoring. Philadelphia closed as a 13.5-point underdog , making this the franchise's largest upset victory in a playoff game in 35 years. "This is who we are. Game 1 was not who we are," Maxey said. Jaylen Brown led all scorers with 36 points but acknowledged the 76ers' young star earned his performance. "I thought Edgecombe was just too comfortable," Brown said. "He's a rookie, but he can play. We've got to be better on him." The Celtics shot 39.3 percent overall and went 13-of-47 from three, committing 13 turnovers that produced 16 Philadelphia points. Jayson Tatum finished with 19 points, 14 rebounds, and nine assists.

title: Duke Lands No. 3 Transfer John Blackwell From Wisconsin
summary: John Blackwell, the third-ranked transfer in this cycle, has committed to Duke, head coach Jon Scheyer announced Tuesday. The former Wisconsin guard averaged 19.1 points, 5.1 rebounds, and 2.3 assists last season, earning All-Big Ten third-team recognition. The 6-foot-4 rising senior is the most significant transfer acquisition of Scheyer's tenure and is expected to slot directly into the starting lineup. Duke projects as a top-five program entering next season. Blackwell was the premier scoring guard available in the portal this spring. He connected on 38.9 percent of 7.3 three-point attempts per game, including 44.2 percent on catch-and-shoot opportunities. On January 10, he dropped 26 points with four three-pointers to hand eventual national champion Michigan its first defeat of the season. His arrival fits a deliberate organizational shift toward experience. Duke recently secured the returns of point guard Cayden Boozer and center Patrick Ngongba II, giving Scheyer multiple veteran pieces despite likely losing forward Cameron Boozer and wing Isaiah Evans to the NBA draft. Blackwell's postseason track record strengthens his case further. He posted 22 points and 10 rebounds in Wisconsin's first-round NCAA Tournament loss to High Point and averaged 26 points across three Big Ten tournament games. Beyond scoring, Blackwell brings reliability at the free-throw line, shooting 86.3 percent last season, and draws fouls at an elite rate, five per 40 minutes, ranking in the top 300 nationally per KenPom. Duke's incoming freshman class remains elite, featuring top-15 prospects Cam Williams, Deron Rippey Jr., and Bryson Howard. With multiple returning starters now joined by a seasoned transfer, the Blue Devils enter next season as a credible national championship contender.

title: Victor Wembanyama Suffers Concussion After Hitting Floor In Game 2 Loss
summary: Victor Wembanyama has been diagnosed with a concussion after he was hurt early in the second quarter of the San Antonio Spurs' 106-103 loss in Game 2 to the Portland Trail Blazers. Wembanyama was driving to the basket against Blazers guard Jrue Holiday when he was fouled, lost his footing and fell forward, striking his head on the floor. He remained down briefly, appeared disoriented while attempting to rise and was ultimately escorted to the locker room for evaluation. Spurs general manager Brian Wright accompanied him. The team ruled him out shortly after Wright returned to the court as he was placed in the concussion protocol. "It's more than a basketball game for us, so I was just trying to check on my brother," Stephon Castle told NBC's Zora Stephenson at halftime. Under NBA concussion protocol, any player showing concussion symptoms must be removed immediately and evaluated in a distraction-free environment. A physician must also review video of the incident before any return to play is permitted. Should Wembanyama receive a formal concussion diagnosis, league rules bar him from playing for a minimum of 48 hours. Game 3 is scheduled for Friday in Portland. Wembanyama posted 35 points in Sunday's Game 1 victory and was named the NBA's Defensive Player of the Year on Monday . Wembanyama sat out 18 regular-season games this year, including all three matchups against Portland. San Antonio went 12-6 without him in the lineup.

title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Wins 2025-26 NBA Clutch Player Of The Year In Near-Unanimous Vote
summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has been named the 2025-26 Kia NBA Clutch Player of the Year, capturing the Jerry West Trophy in near-unanimous fashion with 96 of 100 first-place votes. Gilgeous-Alexander edged Jamal Murray and Anthony Edwards for the honor, with Cade Cunningham and Jalen Brunson rounding out the top five. He is the fourth different winner in the award's four-year history , joining De'Aaron Fox, Stephen Curry and Brunson. The Thunder guard led the entire NBA with 175 total clutch points and 6.5 points per game in situations where games were within five points during the final five minutes of regulation or overtime. His efficiency in critical moments was equally striking. Gilgeous-Alexander converted 60.9 percent of his two-point attempts in clutch time, going 39-of-64, while also recording an NBA-best 16 go-ahead field goals during those situations. Oklahoma City posted a 20-7 record in clutch games when Gilgeous-Alexander played, a .741 winning percentage that underscored his direct impact on close outcomes. Gilgeous-Alexander is also the betting favorite to win NBA MVP .

title: Steve Kerr Unlikely To Remain With Warriors Barring Change Of Heart
summary: Steve Kerr entered the 2025-26 NBA season knowing it would likely be his last with the Golden State Warriors. Following Friday's play-in tournament defeat to the Phoenix Suns, the expectation around the league is that his 12-year run as Warriors head coach is finished. Multiple league sources confirm Kerr arrived at training camp mentally prepared to close out his tenure. Kerr firmly rejected contract extension talks last fall, signaling his intentions early. "I'm very comfortable just going into this season with a year left," Kerr said last September. "I'm so aligned with (general manager Mike Dunleavy) and (CEO Joe Lacob). There's no reason for discussion or concern. This is kind of a point in our relationship where it's like, let's just see how it is at the end of the year." A decision is expected within days rather than weeks. Sources say money will not be the determining factor in his choice. "They could offer Steve $25 million a year," one league source said, "and I doubt that alone would make a difference." Golden State constructed its roster around a single-season push. That plan unraveled through a devastating injury wave as Jimmy Butler missed 44 games following a torn ACL, Stephen Curry sat out 39, and Al Horford missed 37. Kerr deployed 43 different starting lineups in search of stability. Two sources indicate that any reversal would require Kerr feeling genuine confidence in his standing as the franchise transitions toward rebuilding. Kerr went on the record with ESPN saying that he was 50-50 on whether to return . Kerr arrived in Golden State in 2014 and built the NBA's most influential dynasty, introducing the celebrated "Death Lineup" and later winning four championships. The current Warriors roster, depleted and aging, bears little resemblance to those dominant squads.

title: Projected NBA Lottery Pick Thomas Haugh Returns To Florida For Senior Season
summary: Thomas Haugh has decided to return to Florida for his senior season. Haugh was a projected lottery pick in this year's draft had he declared as the 13th ranked prospect. Haugh appeared in 34 games last season with the Gators, averaging 17.1 points, 6.2 rebounds and 2.1 assists. "Most guys in my position in the draft, it would be a no-brainer to go to the NBA," Haugh told ESPN. "It's not just the NIL. It's a chance to play with my boys. To play for coach [Todd] Golden. To go to the school I love to play for. It was definitely a tougher decision than last year, but it was best for my career and future." Haugh is the first projected lottery pick to return to school since Michigan State's Miles Bridges in 2017. Haugh earned third-team All-American honors and first-team All-SEC honors this past season. The idea of Haugh returning for his senior season didn't appear to have any momentum until Haugh's family, and agent Aaron Klevan of THE·TEAM, approached Golden and asked what a potential return would look like in terms of situation and compensation. "They really didn't need to sell much," Haugh said. "Coach Golden and the staff did a great job, not pressuring me. They're my guys. They're gonna text me and call me regardless. They didn't do much recruiting. I grew up a Florida fan. Tim Tebow. The back-to-back national championships. The 2014 team, I remember. They didn't really have to sell me." Haugh figures to make more in NIL money in college than in his pick range of 11-15. "The unique angle that we were able to drive home to Tommy's family and Aaron Klevan, this dude has real bottom-line NIL value," Golden said. "That's an area right now where elite college athletes have an advantage over mid-tier pros. title: Tiago Splitter Remains Leading Candidate For Blazers' Head Coaching Job
summary: Portland Trail Blazers owner Tom Dundon has conducted an extensive coaching search while the team competes in the playoffs, with multiple sources confirming that interim head coach Tiago Splitter remains the front-runner for the permanent position despite widespread interviews with other candidates. Reports suggesting Dundon seeks to fill the role for $1 million to $1.5 million are false, according to team sources. The focus of the search has been identifying the strongest candidate, regardless of cost. "Of all the things reported on Tom, the one thing that is not true is the coaching thing, that he is trying to get someone for $1 million to 1.5 million," a team source told The Athletic. "It's just not true. He's talked to everybody, and of course, some coaches he talks to would be less expensive than others. The goal is to find the best person." Dundon has spoken with St. Louis University coach Josh Schertz, Iowa coach Ben McCollum, former NBA coach Michael Malone prior to his hiring at North Carolina, and former New York Knicks coach Tom Thibodeau, who is no longer under consideration. "Tiago is going to be the leading candidate," a team source said. "People ask: Why don't we just give Tiago the job? That would be the easy thing to do. But ownership wouldn't be doing the team or the fan base justice if other people weren't interviewed." The ongoing search has drawn criticism from league observers. Splitter was thrust into the interim role before the season's second game when Chauncey Billups was arrested by the FBI in a gambling probe. "The amount of disrespect (toward Splitter) that's going on is beyond description," a league source said. "It's like, every day a new name is coming up. It's the most vicious thing I've encountered in 30-plus years." No formal offer has been extended to Splitter as of yet.

title: Rudy Gobert Holds Nikola Jokic To 1-Of-8 Shooting As Wolves Even Series With Nuggets
summary: Rudy Gobert delivered a defensive masterclass against three-time MVP Nikola Jokic on Monday, limiting him to 1-of-8 shooting in direct matchups as the Minnesota Timberwolves defeated the Denver Nuggets 119-114 to level their first-round series at 1-1. The performance marked the worst shooting game of Jokic's playoff career against a single defender, with a minimum of six shot attempts. Minnesota generated 1.157 points per possession compared to Denver's 1.109. The result carried added significance as Gobert placed fourth in Defensive Player of the Year balloting earlier on Monday , finishing behind Chet Holmgren, Ausar Thompson, and unanimous winner Victor Wembanyama. "Not the first time I've gotten disrespected," Gobert said. "Probably not the last. If you want to disrespect greatness, take it for granted, whatever, soon they'll realize the impact." Gobert's impact was most visible in the fourth quarter. Anthony Edwards instructed Gobert to defend Jokic straight up entering the final period, abandoning double-teams. Gobert then forced three straight misses in the closing minutes. "I told him we ain't bringing no double team," Edwards said. "You gonna guard [Jokic] one-on-one. Stop fouling. Stop going for the reach-in. Because he's going to flop. They're going to call the foul. Play him straight up." Jokic finished with 24 points, 14 of which came during a third-quarter stretch when Gobert sat with four fouls. Edwards battled through a right knee problem to post a team-high 30 points in 40 minutes. Julius Randle added 24 points and Donte DiVincenzo contributed 16, including a late three-pointer that provided the decisive margin. Postgame, Jaden McDaniels offered pointed comments about Denver's defense. "Go at Jokic, Jamal, all the bad defenders," McDaniels said. "Tim Hardaway [Jr.], Cam Johnson. Aaron Gordon. Their whole team. They're all bad defenders." Game 3 shifts to Minnesota on Thursday with the Nuggets -1.5 point favorites .

title: Mitchell, Harden, Mobley Power Combine For 83 Points In Cavs' Game 2 Win Over Raptors
summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers' three-headed offensive attack proved too much for Toronto once again. Donovan Mitchell scored 30 points, James Harden added 28, and Evan Mobley contributed 25 as they defeated the Toronto Raptors 115-105 in Game 2 Monday night, taking a 2-0 first-round Eastern Conference series lead. The victory extended Cleveland's postseason winning streak against Toronto to 12 straight, tying an NBA record for consecutive playoff wins against a single opponent. The Cavs hold identical 12-game active streaks against both Detroit and Atlanta. Cleveland generated 1.190 points per possession versus the Raptors' 1.086. The Cavaliers never surrendered the lead throughout the contest. "Your superstars step up," Kenny Atkinson said. "This is a superstar game. Those three led us. They hit the big shots. That's why these guys ... that's why they're stars." Mitchell and Harden combined to score or assist on 78 of Cleveland's 115 points, 68% of the team's total output, per ESPN Research. Atkinson highlighted their floor communication as a critical factor since Harden's mid-season arrival. "They're in constant conversations about what's going on out there in the huddle," Atkinson said. "De facto leaders, right? They're vocal and they see what's going on on both sides of it. ... That's why it helps to have two smart guys like that." Scottie Barnes posted a playoff career-best 26 points for Toronto, while RJ Barrett added 22. Brandon Ingram struggled badly, managing just seven points on 3-for-15 shooting. Both Mitchell and Harden publicly credited reserve forward Dean Wade's defensive work on Ingram as decisive. "As much as y'all talk about us three, Dean Wade deserves a bunch of credit for tonight," Mitchell said. Toronto committed a season-worst 22 turnovers. Game 3 is Thursday in Toronto with the Cavaliers -3.5 point favorites on the road .

title: Billy Donovan Exits Bulls, Plans To Continue NBA Coaching Career
summary: Billy Donovan has decided to step down as head coach of the Chicago Bulls. Donovan had been with the Bulls for the past six seasons since he left the Oklahoma City Thunder in 2020. The Bulls wanted to retain Donovan as head coach despite their front office changes. The Bulls fired executive vice president of basketball operations Arturas Karnisovas and general manager Marc Eversley on April 6. Donovan decided he wanted to allow the Bulls to have a clean break as they search for a new top basketball executive. Donovan met with Bulls ownership over the past week and was given the opportunity to stay as head coach and even in a new managerial capacity. Donovan plans to continue his coaching career and will be one of the leading candidates for teams with vacancies. Donovan received interest from the New York Knicks during the 2025 offseason before signing an extension with the Bulls. The University of North Carolina also wanted to interview Donovan, but eventually moved forward with hiring Michael Malone.

title: Hornets Hope To Re-Sign Coby White This Summer
summary: When the Charlotte Hornets traded for Coby White, GM Jeff Peterson said he envisioned it being a long-term move. Peterson restated those desires at his end-of-season media availability on Monday. "Like I said when we traded for Coby, we envisioned him as somebody who's going to be with the Hornets for a long time," Peterson said about re-signing White. "He embodies what we're about in terms of just of course on the court he's a really, really good player. But the human being, his approach, his professionalism ... I'm just happy for him what he's been able to accomplish in this short amount of time. "I'm excited to see what this offseason looks like. He gets a full off season with us and then going into next season as well." White is set to become an unrestricted free agent on July 1. The Hornets traded Collin Sexton and three second-round picks to acquire White at the trade deadline. That was later amended to two second-round picks, as Charlotte discovered the veteran combo guard was dealing with a left calf injury. In 21 games with the Hornets, White averaged 15.6 points in 19.4 minutes per game off the bench. The seventh-year guard shot 46.1% from the field, including 39.1% on 5.2 three-point attempts per game.

title: Jeff Peterson Won't Take Shortcuts To Push Hornets Forward
summary: Charlotte Hornets GM Jeff Peterson is proud of the progress his team made, but won't take shortcuts to push his team into the playoffs next season. The Hornets fell to the Orlando Magic in the Eastern Conference Play-In Tournament's game for the eighth seed. "We can't skip steps," Peterson said. "Again, I've seen it too many times and it ends up not going the way that you think it goes, in terms of trying to speed things up. Look, we've made a ton of progress this season internally, and I'm excited because I know that's going to continue to happen. But we'll continue to be strategic and when the opportunity presents itself, I know we'll be ready because of the flexibility that we have." Charlotte has two picks in the first round of the 2026 NBA Draft. After selecting four rotation players a year ago, the Hornets have another opportunity to deepen their roster this year. "I'm very excited about this draft," Peterson said. "It's if not the deepest, one of the deepest that I've ever been a part of. A lot of good players all over the draft, of course. But again, where we are from an asset standpoint, it's going to allow us to be flexible. So whether that means we bring two players in, consolidate, we'll have different options that we'll continue to explore and look at. "But I'm excited for whoever we bring in because I know that it's going to be someone who is a Hornet, and about what we're about and at some point will contribute to what we are building here."

title: CJ McCollum After Scoring 32 Points In Hawks' Game 2 Win: 'I'm No Villain'
summary: CJ McCollum finished with 32 points and six assists in the Atlanta Hawks' 107-106 win over the New York Knicks in Game 2 to even the series. McCollum scored the go-ahead step-back jumper over OG Anunoby with 34 seconds left in the game to secure the win. "I'm no villain, I'm a nice guy with two kids and a wife," McCollum said. "I think it's admiration. Great, passionate fans in a hostile environment. It's fun, It's basketball, it's the playoffs. If anything, I think it's a sign of respect." McCollum missed two free throws with 5.6 seconds left in the game and the Hawks up one. Miles Bridges missed a fadeaway jumper at the buzzer. "When the buzzer sounds and it's quiet and you walk off the court, there's a level of mutual respect," McCollum said. "I've been to games in the Garden as a fan ... I've seen playoff games here. It's a pleasure to be able to play here. A pleasure to walk off the court with a win." Jalen Johnson added 17 points for the Hawks. Jalen Brunson finished with 29 points for the Knicks, with Karl-Anthony Towns adding 18 points and Josh Hart scoring 15 points. The Knicks are -1.5 point favorites for Game 3 at the Hawks on Thursday .

title: Al Horford Optimistic He'll Decide To Continue Playing Next Season
summary: Golden State Warriors center Al Horford said Monday he will spend the next several weeks deciding whether to exercise his player option worth nearly $6 million for next season while leaving open the possibility of retirement. "I think that, for me, it has to do with not rushing to anything or not let emotions or heat of the moment determine anything," Horford said. "I just think I've done this the last few years, and it's just kind of taken time to step back and have a clear picture, look at the outlook of the team, looking at the future of the team, how things look, and also seeing kind of where I'm at and how I'm feeling." Horford, who turns 40 on June 3, appeared in just 45 games this season due to a series of injuries, averaging 8.3 points and 4.9 rebounds. Despite limited availability, he became a trusted presence in the locker room and a well-regarded teammate. When asked directly whether retirement was under consideration, Horford stopped short of ruling it out while sounding cautiously optimistic about continuing. "Fortunately for me, I'm healthy," he said. "I feel good. I still feel like I can contribute and play at a very high level still. Those are all things that I'm going to look at. Now that everything kind of gets quiet, you know, it's something that I'll reflect on all that." Horford said Golden State's competitive outlook next season will factor heavily into his choice. He noted that his family has settled comfortably in the Bay Area, which could work in the Warriors' favor as they look to retain him.

title: Brandin Podziemski Wants To Remain With Warriors Long-Term Ahead Of Extension Talks
summary: Golden State Warriors guard Brandin Podziemski expressed a strong desire Monday to remain with the franchise that drafted him, saying he hopes to finalize a contract extension before next season's deadline in October. "I want to be here for the long term," Podziemski said during exit interviews. "It's always special having the team that drafted you, so, hopefully, we can get something done." Podziemski said he is confident his agent, Bill Duffy, and the front office can reach an agreement in the coming months. Podziemski completed his third NBA season averaging 13.8 points, 5.1 rebounds and 3.7 assists while appearing in all 82 regular-season games, a mark reached by only 17 other players league-wide. Podziemski acknowledged that watching teammates Moses Moody and Jonathan Kuminga navigate their respective extensions has shaped his own thinking. "I think both seeing him and Moses do two different things for me helped," Podziemski said. "Just seeing the goods and bads to both sides. I don't think one is better than the other, but seeing that as a teammate and seeing how they handled both situations, I think is going to help me." Moody signed a three-year, $39 million extension in October 2024. Kuminga's negotiations stretched into training camp before he agreed to a two-year, $48.5 million deal, ultimately being dealt to the Atlanta Hawks in February for Kristaps Porzingis.

title: Victor Wembanyama Unanimously Named NBA Defensive Player Of The Year
summary: Victor Wembanyama has unanimously been named the 2025-26 NBA Defensive Player of the Year, becoming the youngest player in league history to win the award at the age of 22. Wembanyama was on his way to winning the award last season before he was ruled out for the season in February due to deep vein thrombosis. Wembanyama is the first player in NBA history to receive all first place votes for Defensive Player of the Year. Chet Holmgren finished second with 76 second place votes and 11 third place votes ahead of Ausar Thompson with nine second place votes and 33 third place votes. Rudy Gobert and Scottie Barnes rounded out the top-5. Derrick White, Cason Wallace, Amen Thompson, Dyson Daniels, OG Anunoby, Jalen Duren, Draymond Green and Bam Adebayo also received Defensive Player of the Year votes. Wembanyama led the NBA in blocks for the third straight season at 3.1 per game and 197 for the season. The San Antonio Spurs allowed just 103.6 points per 100 possessions with Wembanyama on the floor compared to 113.7 with him off. Wembanyama is also a top-3 finisher for Most Valuable Player. Wembanyama is the fourth member of the Spurs to be named Defensive Player of the Year, joining Alvin Robertson, David Robinson and Kawhi Leonard (2X). Despite his extended period of defensive excellence, Tim Duncan was never named Defensive Player of the Year.

title: Joel Embiid Starts Recovery Program After Appendectomy, Timeline Uncertain
summary: Philadelphia 76ers center Joel Embiid has begun a strength and conditioning program following his emergency appendectomy nearly two weeks ago, the team announced Monday. However, he remains in Philadelphia and is effectively ruled out for Tuesday's Game 2 against the Boston Celtics. The 76ers dropped Game 1 on Sunday, 123-91, a result that underscored how dependent Philadelphia's offense is on Embiid's presence. The team hopes he can return at some point during the series, though that window may narrow quickly depending on how competitive the Sixers prove to be. Without Embiid, Boston was able to direct greater defensive focus toward Tyrese Maxey and Paul George, neutralizing Philadelphia's halfcourt attack. The Celtics have no reliable matchup for Embiid in isolation, and his absence allowed Boston to apply suffocating pressure elsewhere. Philadelphia responded with a pointed practice and film session Monday. Nick Nurse indicated the session was more targeted than usual. "Normally, we go over film in general on offense and defense and special teams," Nurse said. "Not today. We were much more specific today." The Sixers were visibly disappointed with both their effort and output in Game 1 and expect a sharper performance going forward despite entering the series as underdogs. Embiid appeared in 38 regular season games, averaging just over 26 points per game. Coming off nearly two years of knee problems, he demonstrated he remains among the league's most imposing offensive forces when healthy. The original plan was to manage his workload during the regular season to ensure he was fully available for the playoffs.

The Grizzlies qualified for the playoffs in four of Jenkins' six seasons as head coach. Jenkins was an assistant coach for the Bucks under Mike Budenholzer during the 2018-19 season before he was hired as head coach of the Grizzlies. Jenkins is considered one of the top coaching free agents of the cycle and could wait to see what ends up happening with other potential jobs such as the Orlando Magi. Doc Rivers stepped down as head coach of the Bucks at the end of the season. The Bucks have yet to signal whether they will trade Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason, or continue to pursue an extension.

title: Jazz, Hawks Amongst Six NBA Draft Tiebreaker Winners
summary: Six ties among teams with identical regular-season records were broken today through random drawings to determine the order of selection for NBA Draft 2026. The drawings were conducted by NBA President of League Operations Byron Spruell at the NBA office in Secaucus, New Jersey. The tiebreaker process was overseen by Megan DeCesaris, a partner from the accounting firm of Ernst & Young. The results of the drawings: • The Utah Jazz (22-60) won a tiebreaker with the Sacramento Kings. • The New Orleans Pelicans (26-56) won a tiebreaker with the Dallas Mavericks. The Atlanta Hawks own the draft rights to the Pelicans' pick . • The Phoenix Suns (45-37) won a tiebreaker with the Orlando Magic and the Philadelphia 76ers. Second and third place in the tiebreaker drawings went to Philadelphia and Orlando, respectively. • The Toronto Raptors (46-36) won a tiebreaker with the Atlanta Hawks. • The Houston Rockets (52-30) won a tiebreaker with the Cleveland Cavaliers. • The New York Knicks (53-29) won a tiebreaker with the Los Angeles Lakers. Utah is guaranteed to keep their first round pick that is top-eight protected and is now extinguished . NBA Draft Lottery 2026 will be held on Sunday, May 10 and air live on ABC at 3:00 p.m. ET. LA Clippers → Oklahoma City 13. Miami 14. Charlotte The order for the remainder of the first round picks is as follows: 15. Portland → Chicago 16. Phoenix → Memphis via Orlando 17. Philadelphia → Oklahoma City 18. Orlando → Charlotte via Phoenix 19. Toronto 20. Atlanta → San Antonio 21. Minnesota → Detroit 22. Houston → Philadelphia via Oklahoma City 23. Cleveland → Atlanta 24. title: Draymond Green Says It 'Feels Like' Steve Kerr Has Coached His Last Game With Warriors
summary: Draymond Green publicly predicted Monday that Steve Kerr's 12-year run as Golden State Warriors head coach has come to an end, three days after Kerr himself acknowledged uncertainty about his future with the franchise. "I hope he's our coach next season," Green said on his podcast, "The Draymond Green Show." "You want my opinion? I think not. Just because it feels like that. It felt like that was it." The comments followed an emotional scene on the Phoenix arena floor after the Warriors were eliminated Friday night. Kerr gathered Green and Stephen Curry on the sideline, with microphones capturing him telling the two: "I don't know what's going to happen next, but I love you guys to death. Thank you." Kerr said Friday he plans to spend the next week or two weighing his future. No significant discussions took place over the weekend. Exit interviews with players were held Monday at the team's San Francisco facility, with substantial conversations between Kerr, general manager Mike Dunleavy and controlling owner Joe Lacob anticipated in the coming days. Kerr has placed his own return odds at roughly 50-50, per ESPN. Team sources indicate owner Lacob wants to see genuine enthusiasm from Kerr about continuing - not a reluctant commitment rooted in loyalty to Curry and Green. If Kerr does return, management prefers a multiyear contract rather than a farewell-season arrangement driven by nostalgia. "I still love coaching, but I get it," Kerr said Friday. "These jobs all have an expiration date. There is a run that happens, and when the run ends, sometimes it's time for new blood and new ideas." Green's own future in Golden State remains unsettled. He holds a one-year, $27.6 million player option and is uncertain about his path forward. Curry is entering the final season of his contract, though both sides have expressed mutual interest in an extension. "I've never been so uncertain since earlier in my career in what happens next," Green said. "But I'm truly at a loss now because you don't know what direction will be next. ... I also hope I'm on this team next season. We also don't know that. Man, if it was, what a run."

title: Bulls Identify Six Candidates In President Of Basketball Operations Search
summary: The Chicago Bulls have launched their search for a new head of basketball operations, receiving permission to interview five front office executives from around the league, sources told ESPN. Agent Austin Brown is also expected to be considered for the position. Interviews are scheduled to begin this week. The organization is targeting a hire shortly before or after the mid-May draft combine. The vacancy was created April 6 when the Bulls dismissed executive vice president Arturas Karnisovas and general manager Marc Eversley following six years that produced just one playoff appearance. Bulls CEO Michael Reinsdorf has been direct that a commitment to retaining head coach Billy Donovan is the one requirement for any incoming decision-maker. "If I interview someone and they're not sold on Billy, they're not sold on a Hall of Fame coach; they're not sold on a person who's won championships in college, who's gone deep in the playoffs with Oklahoma City," Reinsdorf said April 7. "... If Billy wants to be our coach and someone's not interested in that, then they're probably not the right candidate for us." The five candidates granted interview permission are Minnesota Timberwolves GM Matt Lloyd, Detroit Pistons senior vice president Dennis Lindsey, Atlanta Hawks senior vice president Bryson Graham, Cleveland Cavaliers GM Mike Gansey and San Antonio Spurs assistant GM Dave Telep. Lloyd brings 17 years of prior Chicago experience, while Lindsey has three decades of front office and scouting work, including seven years as Utah Jazz general manager. Graham rose from intern to GM over 15 seasons with the New Orleans Pelicans. Gansey helped rebuild Cleveland into an Eastern Conference contender, and Telep spent 13 seasons in San Antonio's front office after a career as a prominent recruiting analyst. Brown, a Chicago native and co-head of CAA's basketball division, currently represents prominent players including Donovan Mitchell, Trae Young and Cooper Flagg. He was previously offered the Bulls' GM role during the 2020 search. Three agents, Bob Myers, Rob Pelinka and Leon Rose, have successfully transitioned to top NBA front office positions over the past 15 years.
